target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts, followup messages queued during an active agent turn are silently lost.

## Solution

Persist followup queues in shared SQLite (`followup_queue_entries`), migrate legacy JSON sidecars via doctor, rehydrate from live config, and wake restored routes on gateway startup so production idle-kick can drain them.

Classify `followup-queue-restore` heartbeats as wake payloads so empty/comment-only `HEARTBEAT.md` cannot skip restored-queue system events before drain.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Followup messages queued during an active Telegram turn survive gateway restart and can be delivered afterward without silent loss; restored-queue recovery heartbeats must bypass `HEARTBEAT.md` idle gates.
- Real environment tested: Local machine `joseph-MS-7B51`, systemd `openclaw-gateway.service`, Telegram DM chatId `6300969793`, sessionKey `agent:main:telegram:slash:6300969793`; prior live proof on build `920cc1153`, current-head validation on `b22167a7f` (wake-classification fix).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build` in `~/dev/openclaw`; `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway` (live path, prior head).
  2. Stop gateway; seed `followup_queue_entries` with marker `LIVE_PROOF_TG_1780458928`; `systemctl --user start openclaw-gateway`; `openclaw agent --session-key agent:main:telegram:slash:6300969793 --to 6300969793 --channel telegram --deliver --message "<read oldest pending followup>"`.
  3. Current head (`b22167a7f`): `pnpm exec vitest run` on queue/gateway/heartbeat/migration shards (see Tests section).
- Evidence after fix (redacted runtime log / copied live output):
  - Journal: `[followup-queue-recovery] requested heartbeat wake for 1 restored followup queue route(s)`
  - Journal: `[telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=6300969793 messageId=4122` and `4123`
  - Agent JSON payload text: `Telegram live restart proof: reply with exactly LIVE_PROOF_TG_1780458928`, `deliverySucceeded: true`
  - SQLite row length 864 unchanged immediately after restart (sessionId `7bb5f88d-6d65-4497-a6f3-bda08aafd33f`)
  - Artifacts: `~/.openclaw/workspace/logs/followup-live-proof-final-20260603T035500Z/`
  - Current-head Vitest (no mocks on SQLite round-trip): `queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts` exercises mid-turn Telegram enqueue (`restartIfIdle=false`), simulated SIGKILL restart, restore, exactly-once redelivery with routing intact, and no replay after second restart.
  - Current-head Vitest (heartbeat gates): empty and legacy-comment-only `HEARTBEAT.md` + `source: followup-queue-restore` + queued restore system event → heartbeat runs (not `empty-heartbeat-file` skip).
- Observed result after fix: Restored followup prompt text was read from persisted queue and delivered to Telegram after gateway restart; transport and gateway healthy after rebuild. On current head, recovery requests `source: followup-queue-restore` / `reason: restored-followup-queue` and heartbeat preflight treats that source as a wake payload.
- What was not tested: Full live Telegram mid-turn ingress (message #2 while turn #1 running) re-run on current head `b22167a7f`; that path is covered in-process by `queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts` against real shared SQLite. Mantis native Telegram capture not run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Live proof used controlled SQLite seed plus agent turn to read/deliver restored prompt on prior head; production mid-turn enqueue, idle-kick drain, per-key isolation, and wake-classification regressions are covered by Vitest on current head.

## Tests and validation

Current head `b22167a7f` (local, 2026-06-04):

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/gateway/server-followup-queue-recovery.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-wake.test.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue.drain-restart.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts \
  src/infra/followup-queue-sqlite.test.ts \
  src/infra/state-migrations.test.ts
```

Result: **9 files, 102 tests passed**.

CI: mergeable with upstream `main`; `build-artifacts` and OpenGrep green on latest push.

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts

## Problem

When the gateway process restarts, followup messages queued during an active agent turn are silently lost.

## Solution

Persist followup queues in shared SQLite (`followup_queue_entries`), migrate legacy JSON sidecars via doctor, rehydrate from live config, and wake restored routes on gateway startup so production idle-kick can drain them.

Classify `followup-queue-restore` heartbeats as wake payloads so empty/comment-only `HEARTBEAT.md` cannot skip restored-queue system events before drain.
